cases.title: Scenario matrix row for check-in latency median under 30 seconds
preconditions: Testing-plan §7.1 performance tests and §11 NFR verification matrix rows for NFR-01 and AC-20 apply.
Representative pilot load fixture is seeded: multiple open class sessions with enrolled students and valid rotating QR tokens.
Telemetry or test harness can record per-attempt elapsed time from check-in command start to terminal outcome persistence.
steps: Execute a representative pilot load run across multiple class sessions with enrolled students submitting valid check-ins.
For each attempt, record end-to-end elapsed time from QR/token resolution through POST /v1/check-ins to persisted Success outcome.
Aggregate elapsed times and compute median across all successful check-in attempts in the run.
cases.expected: Median student check-in time across the pilot load run is strictly below 30 seconds, satisfying AC-20 and NFR-01 per testing-plan §7.1 and §11 evidence requirements.

cases.title: Check-in service DB transaction completes within server-side latency budget
preconditions: Real database transaction boundary is enabled for check-in service and attendance ledger modules.
Open class session with valid QR token exists; authenticated enrolled student has no prior success for the session.
Server-side timing instrumentation captures request receipt through CheckInAttempt and AttendanceRecord commit.
steps: Submit POST /v1/check-ins with valid qrToken, clientTimestamp, Idempotency-Key, and student bearer token.
Measure server-side elapsed time from API handler entry to DB commit of CheckInAttempt outcome=Success and AttendanceRecord Present or Late.
Repeat for a statistically meaningful sample under normal (non-degraded) integration test conditions.
cases.expected: Server-side check-in command processing (validation through attendance ledger write) completes well within the 30-second end-to-end budget; sample median server processing time is a small fraction of the AC-20 gate so client and network overhead remain within overall median target.

cases.title: Concurrent class-start burst preserves median check-in time below 30 seconds
preconditions: Multiple open class sessions each have 20+ enrolled students per representative pilot profile.
Test harness can fire parallel POST /v1/check-ins requests across sessions with distinct students and valid QR tokens.
Normal campus network simulation profile is active (no artificial throttling beyond pilot realism).
steps: Trigger simultaneous check-in submissions across concurrent open sessions mimicking class-start burst.
Record per-student elapsed time from submission start to Success response and persisted attendance row.
Compute median elapsed time across all successful attempts in the burst run.
cases.expected: Under concurrent class-start peak load, median check-in time remains below 30 seconds and valid requests maintain processing success without violating NFR-04 concurrent handling expectations tied to AC-20.

cases.title: POST /v1/check-ins returns 200 success envelope within latency budget
preconditions: Open class session with valid qrToken exists in test environment.
Authenticated student bearer token belongs to an actively enrolled user without prior success for the session.
E2E harness records HTTP round-trip time and response envelope fields.
steps: Start timer at POST /v1/check-ins request dispatch with Authorization, valid qrToken, clientTimestamp, and Idempotency-Key.
Await HTTP response and stop timer on full response body receipt.
Verify HTTP status 200, standard envelope (data/meta/error), data.outcome Success, and data.attendanceStatus Present or Late.
cases.expected: API returns HTTP 200 with valid success envelope; measured round-trip time for each attempt in the sample contributes to a median below 30 seconds per AC-20 and API-G05 mobile-first low-latency check-in goal.

cases.title: WF-03 primary student check-in happy path completes within 30-second median target
preconditions: Lecturer has opened session (AC-01); session is Open with rotating valid QR.
Student actor is enrolled; GPS policy is default or satisfied for the session.
E2E harness measures wall-clock time from QR scan/deep-link open through success confirmation.
steps: Student opens check-in URL with valid token (FLOW-01 step 1); complete login gate if unauthenticated (AC-06).
Grant GPS when prompted if policy requires; submit check-in via POST /v1/check-ins.
Record elapsed time until success response and Present or Late attendance is queryable on roster.
Repeat across representative sample of students and sessions; compute median elapsed time.
cases.expected: Primary WF-03 happy-path median end-to-end time from QR scan to success result is below 30 seconds, matching AC-20 Given/When/Then and WF-03 UX latency checkpoint (NFR-01).

cases.title: Median check-in time threshold boundary fails gate when at or above 30 seconds
preconditions: Performance test environment can simulate degraded conditions (elevated latency or reduced capacity) for threshold validation.
Representative pilot load script records per-attempt timings and computes median.
Release gate documentation requires explicit failure evidence when AC-20 is not met.
steps: Run pilot load scenario under controlled degradation until observed median approaches or exceeds 30 seconds.
Capture metric snapshot: per-attempt times, computed median, session count, and success rate.
Compare median against AC-20 threshold and document gate pass/fail verdict.
cases.expected: When computed median is >= 30 seconds, AC-20 gate is marked FAIL with documented metric evidence; when median < 30 seconds under normal pilot profile, gate is PASS. Boundary validates measurability of the 30-second acceptance threshold per NFR-01.

cases.title: FLOW-01 mobile-web journey from QR open to CheckInResultScreen success under 30 seconds
preconditions: Student is on mobile-web viewport (Chromium mobile emulation) with open session and valid check-in deep link.
Student is pre-authenticated or will complete PG-01 login during the timed journey.
Browser harness records wall-clock from navigation to /check-in?token=... through success-present or success-late UI state.
steps: Navigate to check-in URL with valid token query parameter (FLOW-01 step 1).
Complete login redirect if required (AC-06); grant GPS prompt if policy requires.
Submit check-in and wait for CheckInResultScreen to render success status and timestamp (AC-11, AC-UI-03).
Record elapsed time; repeat for sample runs and compute median.
cases.expected: UI journey median completion from QR/deep-link open to success result screen is below 30 seconds per FLOW-01 timing target (AC-20, NFR-01); confirmation is visible without lecturer page reload.

cases.title: Pilot campus network timing on physical mobile browsers
preconditions: Staging pilot uses real iOS Safari or Android Chrome on campus Wi-Fi or cellular per testing-plan §6.2 browser matrix.
Open session with valid QR displayed; enrolled student device available for timed FLOW-01 run.
Physical device and campus network are not reproducible in Playwright MCP harness.
steps: Student scans classroom QR on physical mobile device under normal campus network conditions.
Complete authentication and check-in through mobile browser to success confirmation.
Record elapsed time; aggregate median across pilot cohort check-ins during class sessions.
cases.expected: Physical pilot median check-in time is below 30 seconds under real campus network conditions, providing production-like evidence for AC-20 beyond emulated harness runs.

cases.title: Non-Student roles denied POST /v1/check-ins per permission matrix
preconditions: Open class session with valid qrToken exists in test environment.
Lecturer and DepartmentAdmin users have valid bearer tokens per permission matrix.
Enrolled Student bearer token is available as authorized control for latency baseline.
steps: Lecturer calls POST /v1/check-ins with valid qrToken and Authorization header.
DepartmentAdmin calls POST /v1/check-ins with the same qrToken context.
Enrolled Student calls POST /v1/check-ins as authorized control and record round-trip time.
Verify denied responses return before attendance ledger mutation and Student control succeeds within latency budget.
cases.expected: Lecturer and DepartmentAdmin receive 403 Forbidden per permission matrix (Submit QR check-in Deny); no CheckInAttempt or AttendanceRecord is created for non-student actors; Student control receives HTTP 200 Success within the AC-20 median latency target so unauthorized traffic does not pollute valid check-in timing evidence.
